questions.question: Use the full form: I {a1} my homework yet

a1.verb_hint: Use have not + past participle for I/you/we/they.

variants: Use the full form: I {a1} my homework yet

questions.question: Use the full form: She {a1} her room yet

a1.verb_hint: Use has not + past participle for he/she/it.

variants: Use the full form: She {a1} her room yet

a1.verb_hint: Put not after have/has.
Never makes the Present Perfect negative without not.
Use not yet for an expected action that has not happened.

questions.question: She {a1} the note; do not use saw

a1.verb_hint: After has not use the past participle, not past simple.

variants: She {a1} the note; do not use saw

questions.question: Use the contracted form: We {a1} the book yet

a1.verb_hint: Use the contracted form haven't + past participle.

variants: Use the contracted form: We {a1} the book yet

questions.question: Use the contracted form: She {a1} the email yet

a1.verb_hint: Use the contracted form hasn't + past participle.

variants: Use the contracted form: She {a1} the email yet

a1.verb_hint: Keep not after have/has and before the participle.
Never goes between have/has and the past participle.
Yet normally goes at the end of a negative Present Perfect sentence.
Use gone after hasn't, not went.
